name: documentation-guide
description: Operating manual for the documentation-template framework — writing markdown, configuring site.yaml/navbar.yaml/footer.yaml, working with the issue tracker, blog posts, themes, and anything under the project's data/ folder. Ships 2 skills: documentation-guide (triages tasks to domain-specific reference files — writing, docs-layout, blog-layout, issues, settings-layout, images) and doc-agent (a thin execution-time skill for using an issue's agent-log/ — triggers on audit/refactor/loop, always-on agent-memory, and explicit-save-only discussion). Plus 28 CLI commands behind one manifest-driven dispatcher, each usable as `docs <group> <verb>` or a flat `docs-*` alias, with a uniform contract (--help/-h, --json, exit codes): issue tracker (docs-list/show/subtasks/agent-logs/set-state/add-comment/add-agent-log/review-queue), validators (docs-check-blog/config/section/issues/skill-links), docs+blog content (docs-doc-list/show/search, docs-blog-list/show/search), git-derived metadata (docs-git-updated/changed/log/commit, guarded), cross-content search (docs-find), docs-move for link-aware moves, docs-img to optimize images, docs-help for discovery, and docs-resolve-context. Plus 2 slash commands (/docs-init to bootstrap a new docs project, /docs-add-section to scaffold a new top-level section). Cross-platform CLI: bash + .cmd shims for Linux/macOS/WSL and native Windows; polyglot-ready dispatcher.
